text: **Kelly:** Oh!
**Kelly:** Anna!
**Kelly:** There you are!
**Kelly:** I am so glad we joined the {great|素晴らしい/大きな|/greɪt/|Great job today!} city bird count, aren't you?

**Anna:** No, today has been a {bust|失敗/がっかり|/bʌst/|Today was a total bust.}, a {flop|失敗/駄目なもの|/flɒp/|The movie was a complete flop.}, a {bummer|がっかりするもの/残念なこと|/ˈbʌmər/|What a bummer, we missed the concert.}
**Anna:** I'm sensing a little {disappointment|失望/がっかり|/ˌdɪsəˈpɔɪntmənt/|Her disappointment was clear on her face.}
**Anna:** A little?
**Anna:** Well, my official bird watching
**Anna:** form is almost full.
**Anna:** I've only seen {pigeons|ハト|/ˈpɪdʒənz/|Pigeons are common in city parks.}
**Anna:** Lots and lots of pigeons.
**Anna:** But I did write down some other {valuable|価値のある/重要な|/ˈvæljuəbəl/|This is a valuable piece of information.} information.
**Kelly:** Did you find a bird's nest?
**Anna:** No!
**Anna:** I found ten {ice cream|アイスクリーム|/aɪs kriːm/|Ice cream is a popular summer treat.} trucks in this five-block area.
**Anna:** Their music seems to {follow|追いかける/後を追う|/ˈfɑːloʊ/|The dog followed its owner.} me everywhere.
**Anna:** We're supposed to be counting birds, not ice cream trucks.
**Anna:** This is important {scientific|科学的な|/saɪənˈtɪfɪk/|Scientific research helps us understand the world.} work.
**Anna:** Well, when you want ice cream, my data will be pretty important.
**Kelly:** Anna, birds are everywhere.
**Kelly:** I'm sure you'll find something.
**Kelly:** Use your {imagination|想像力|/ɪˌmædʒɪˈneɪʃən/|Use your imagination to solve this problem.}
**Anna:** But make sure that you put it on the official form.
**Kelly:** Anna was supposed to find birds.
**Anna:** But she didn't, Kelly says.
**Anna:** We're supposed to be counting birds, not ice cream trucks.
**Anna:** To be {supposed|予定されている/期待されている|/səˈpoʊzd/|I am supposed to meet her at 5 pm.} to means you are expected to do something.
**Anna:** It's different from have to and on to.
**Anna:** Have to means you must do something, and on to means you should do it, but you don't have to.
**Anna:** Keep watching for have to.
**Anna:** Two and up to.
**Anna:** No {birds|鳥|/bɜːrdz/|Birds fly in the sky.}
**Anna:** Oh wait, there's another {ice cream truck|アイスクリーム販売車|/aɪs kriːm trʌk/|The ice cream truck plays music.}
**Anna:** Anna {focus|集中する|/ˈfoʊkəs/|I focus on my homework.} on birds.
**Anna:** Oh, but that truck has my {favorite|大好きな|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite book.} flavor.
**Anna:** Banana.
**Anna:** That man is in the way.
**Anna:** I can't see the {price|価格|/praɪs/|The price is too high.}
**Anna:** I wonder what {flavor|風味|/ˈfleɪvər/|What flavor of ice cream?} he's getting.
**Anna:** Strawberry.
**Anna:** He doesn't seem like a strawberry kind of guy.
**Anna:** In fact, he doesn't seem like an ice cream truck kind of guy.
**Anna:** That {suit|スーツ|/suːt/|He wears a black suit.} looks {expensive|高価な|/ɪkˈspensɪv/|This watch is expensive.}
**Anna:** Maybe he's a {spy|スパイ|/spaɪ/|The spy works secretly.}
**Anna:** You know what that means.
**Anna:** The ice cream truck driver is a spy too.
**Anna:** Genius.
**Anna:** It's a {perfect|完璧な|/ˈpɜːrfɪkt/|That's a perfect plan.} {disguise|偽装|/dɪsˈɡaɪz/|He wears a disguise.}
**Anna:** What?
**Anna:** The man in the suit left his {briefcase|ブリーフケース|/ˈbriːfkeɪs/|He carries a brown briefcase.} at the ice cream truck.
**Anna:** I was right.
**Anna:** He is a s
**Anna:** {spy|スパイ|/spaɪ/|The spy works secretly.} and he just dropped off {top secret|極秘の|/tɒp ˈsiːkrət/|Top secret documents.} information.
**Anna:** I {ought|〜すべきだ|/ɔːt/|I ought to help him.} to do something.
**Anna:** Something needs to be done.
**Anna:** I need to do something.
**Anna:** I did it
**Anna:** I took the spy's briefcase.
**Anna:** What's that?
**Anna:** The ice cream truck!
**Anna:** It's {following|追いかける/後をつける|/ˈfɑːloʊɪŋ/|The cat is following me home.} me!
**Anna:** It wants the {case|ケース/箱|/keɪs/|I put my phone in a hard case.} back.
**Anna:** I have to {hide|隠れる/身を隠す|/haɪd/|Children hide during hide-and-seek.}
**Anna:** This is my life now.
**Anna:** {Running|走る/逃げる|/ˈrʌnɪŋ/|I am running in the park.}, {scared|怖がる/恐れる|/skɛrd/|The child was scared of the dark.}, alone.
**Anna:** That {awful|ひどい/恐ろしい|/ˈɔːfəl/|That was an awful movie.} music.
**Anna:** It's following me everywhere.
**Man:** Hey, you found my {briefcase|書類かばん|/ˈbriːfkeɪs/|He carried a black briefcase.}.
**Anna:** You caught me!
**Anna:** What?
**Anna:** I mean I caught you!
**Man:** Well, I just want my briefcase.
**Man:** All my {poems|詩|/ˈpoʊəmz/|She writes beautiful poems.} are in there.
**Man:** Poems?
**Anna:** You're a {poet|詩人|/ˈpoʊət/|He is a famous poet.}?
**Anna:** Well, I'm a {lawyer|弁護士|/ˈlɔːjər/|She works as a lawyer.}, but I write poems too.
**Anna:** So you're not a {spy|スパイ/諜報員|/spaɪ/|The spy worked for the government.}?
**Man:** You look {disappointed|がっかりした|/ˌdɪsəˈpɔɪntɪd/|She looked disappointed by the news.}.
**Anna:** No, that's okay.
**Anna:** It's not your fault.
**Man:** Poems are nice too, I guess.
**Man:** Well, would you like me to read my latest one?
**Anna:** Why not?
**Man:** On sunny day walks, my hand reaches for {ice cream|アイスクリーム|/ˈaɪs kriːm/|We ate ice cream on a hot day.} from {Frageria|架空の地名}.
**Anna:** Love your poem.
**Man:** I love ice cream.
**Anna:** Me too.
**Man:** What's your favorite {flavor|味/風味|/ˈfleɪvər/|Chocolate is my favorite flavor.}?
**Anna:** Banana.
**Man:** Well my favorite flavor is {strawberry|いちご|/ˈstrɔːberi/|Strawberry is a sweet fruit.}.
**Anna:** How'd you {know|知る/分かる|/noʊ/|I know the answer.} that?
**Anna:** A little {bird|鳥|/bɜːrd/|A bird is flying in the sky.} {told|告げる/言った|/toʊld/|She told me a secret.} me
